task_completion.description: Open the chinook.odb database in LibreOffice Base
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreOffice Base main window is open, and the title bar or database pane indicates 'chinook.odb'.

In the LibreOffice Base window, under the 'Tables' (as a View) or 'Queries' section, there is an item named 'TracksNotInPlaylists'. Alternatively, an SQL editor window is open showing the CREATE VIEW or SELECT statement for this query with columns TrackId, Name, and Composer.

In the LibreOffice Base window, under the 'Tables' (as a View) or 'Queries' section, there is an item named 'GenresAboveAvgTrackCount'. Alternatively, an SQL editor window is open showing the CREATE VIEW or SELECT statement for this query with columns GenreId, Name, and TrackCount.

In the LibreOffice Base window, under the 'Tables' (as a View) or 'Queries' section, there is an item named 'HighValueCustomers'. Alternatively, an SQL editor window is open showing the CREATE VIEW or SELECT statement for this query with columns CustomerId, FirstName, LastName, Email, and TotalSpent.

integrity.description: The agent used LibreOffice Base to interact with the database
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the LibreOffice Base GUI being used to create the views or queries, rather than just a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent wrote actual SQL queries for the views
integrity.visual_evidence: If the SQL editor or query design view is visible, it should contain genuine SQL keywords (SELECT, FROM, WHERE, etc.) and table names (Track, Genre, Customer, etc.), not just dummy text or empty tables.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The database is a LibreOffice Base file (/home/ga/chinook.odb) containing 11 tables including Track, Genre, Customer, Invoice, and PlaylistTrack. The expected views/queries are 'TracksNotInPlaylists' (requires NOT IN/NOT EXISTS), 'GenresAboveAvgTrackCount' (requires AVG, GROUP BY, HAVING), and 'HighValueCustomers' (requires SUM, AVG). The created views should be visible in the LibreOffice Base interface under 'Tables' (as Views) or 'Queries'.
